inline_comments.message:
cast to string:

AAIResourceUri sliceProfileUri = AAIUriFactory.createResourceUri(AAIFluentTypeBuilder.business().customer(execution.getVariable("globalSubscriberId") as String).serviceSubscription(execution.getVariable("subscriptionServiceType") as String).serviceInstance(ANNF_sliceProfileInstanceId))

casting:

AAIResourceUri nssiServiceUri = AAIUriFactory.createResourceUri(AAIFluentTypeBuilder.business().customer(execution.getVariable("globalSubscriberId") as String).serviceSubscription(execution.getVariable("subscriptionServiceType") as String).serviceInstance(execution.getVariable("RANServiceInstanceId") as String))

Two issues here:
1. Now the enumeration that you use here, is not available, you have to make an import:
   import org.onap.aaiclient.client.aai.AAINamespaceConstants

2. Access to AAIObjectType exceeds its access rights. This object is protected in its class and it is not accessible here.
Where is object created for this reference? For now there are no candidates for this method call.
I think you have to put an argument here.

For the ServiceInfo object, use setters to set the value of its field. 
The fields of ServiceInfo are private, there are no accessible this way.
For example, make:
serviceInfo.setGlobalSubscriberId(globalSubscriberId) instead of serviceInfo.globalSubscriberId = globalSubscriberId
To do what? If you use this statement, describe please what is to do.

The same as above:
For the ServiceInfo object, use setters to set the value of its field. 
The fields of ServiceInfo are private, there are no accessible this way.
For example, make:
serviceInfo.setGlobalSubscriberId(globalSubscriberId) instead of serviceInfo.globalSubscriberId = globalSubscriberId
You made the changes in this method. I do not see this method is used anywhere. So, it should be removed.
